Hazardous gases may cause a major problem prior to or during the time the sampling crew performs its checks.

impact_notes: Potential loss of orbiter and personnel due to undetected toxic/flammable gas accumulation in compartments post-landing.

evidence.quote: Failure of a critical item lifting device (crane, hydraset, or lifting beam) may result in serious injury/loss of personnel or flight hardware.
Personnel work under a suspended static load while processing SRM's and while the orbiter is in the vertical position to remove landing gear pins.
Modify lifting devices to use DC motors to eliminate erratic trolley and bridge movement.

impact_notes: Risk of serious injury or loss of personnel and flight hardware during routine processing and stacking operations at KSC.

evidence.quote: Failure of switches 3 or 4 results in bridge movement and bridge brake will not engage if a BR-1 relay failure occurs.
Failure of the RTGF crane electrical components, due to an internal malfunction, increases the potential for damage to load and/or crane.

impact_notes: Potential for load damage and crane damage during lifting operations of radioisotope thermoelectric generators; safety-critical failure modes in brake engagement across three independent subsystems (bridge, trolley, hoist).

evidence.quote: A component failure within the oxygen analyzer due to fatigue/shorting may cause an erroneous high output signal when the oxygen level is below 19.5%, resulting in no alarm warning.

impact_notes: Potential asphyxiation/death of personnel due to undetected oxygen deficiency in critical ground facilities (orbiter processing, launch pad, mobile launch platform).

evidence.quote: A design certification review was performed on the platforms. The review identified three non-compliances to design standards as givin by KSC-STD-Z-0002B
only one holding brake...A failure in the hoisting system during raising/lowering could cause the platform to tip, resulting in damage/loss of a SSME
no emergency stop switch, (2) no final upper limit switch, and (3) only one holding brake

impact_notes: Potential loss of space shuttle main engines (SSME), ground support equipment (GSE), or mobile launch platform (MLP) due to platform tipping from hoisting system failure.

evidence.quote: Configuration of the portable hypergol checkout panels hinders emergency egress from platforms 6E and 6W in the OPF.

flexhoses and electrical cables that become entangled and cannot easily be maintained in an orderly arrangement. The flexhoses and cables also present a tripping hazard and further hinder emergency egress

evidence.quote: Lack of relief valve isolation has resulted in excessive toxic vapor releases to the environment.

28 gallons compared to the one gallon expected...extensive dispersion of the vapor cloud exceeded the predesignated clear area of 700 ft.

evidence.quote: Undetected loss of GN2 purge to OMS engine injector cavities could eventually corrode the vehicle system.

evidence.quote: RCS thruster valve seats have a tendency to relax at low temperatures and low pressure, allowing liquid/vapor to leak past the seals.

ensure that dual valve isolation is maintained

evidence.quote: Structural failure of pod bellows or other assembly components could cause onboard reactants to be contaminated with gaseous helium and particulates.

evidence.quote: Improperly grounding or a lack of grounding may cause static electricity build-up and electrical sparks which could act as an ignition source for any flammable vapors present.

evidence.quote: This cover has a butyl rubber o-ring which acts as a moisture seal, but is incompatible with N2O4.

evidence.quote: The -1 and -2 hypergol spill aspirators vent into the atmosphere when used...now identified to operate only at unconfined areas because of their venting characteristics

evidence.quote: The pneumatic and fluid quick disconnects are the same size and located close enough to easily allow misconnection within APU carrier plates.

Could result in injury to personnel.

evidence.quote: The deceleration unit has failed twice. These failures were caused by a low fluid level in the shock absorber.

evidence.quote: A design oversight limits LRU lifting operations to one location, level 19 of the OPF. An extended diving board interferes with the direct decent of flight hardware to the floor.

evidence.quote: With no mechanical locking device to support the bucket load when cables are disconnected for inspection or replacement, a possibility exists for damage or injury.

evidence.quote: exposes personnel to a high pressure source and could result in injury or flight hardware damage...personnel will be in the vicinity of high-pressure fluid, water, and rust inhibitor at 11,600 psig

evidence.quote: workers installing the unit, when faced with a six inch difference in the tank piping from the unit inlet, decided to modify the piping

evidence.quote: There are no brakes on the bridge bucket drive units to ensure the bridge will stop if the drive chain or the electric brake fails.

evidence.quote: Lack of a breaking system on the winches used to lift the FRCS mezzanine platform could result in a mishap if a winch fails.

evidence.quote: Vibration and movement on workstands causes debris shields to move out of place, leaving flight hardware unprotected.

evidence.quote: transportation design requirements for the transportation support equipment exceeded the capabilities of the railcars

evidence.quote: The LO2 and LH2 vent and relief valve position indicator switch tolerance allows the valve to indicate "closed" when it may be open up to 0.30 inch.

evidence.quote: lack of timely closures recorded on completed work items; poor annotation of the steps taken in performing authorized deviations; missing signatures and quality control stamps

evidence.quote: An integrated materials compatibility assessment would have identified this incompatibility issue.

evidence.quote: Stress calculations made prior to the mishap indicated an acceptable factor of safety. However, the assumptions in the calculations for stress neglected to consider the drag force on a flat plate which is a multiplier to the dynamic pressur

evidence.quote: High-voltage circuits designed to operate within the Martian atmosphere require special design precautions, such as insulation from ground potential, to avoid corona or other forms of high-voltage breakdown.

impact_notes: Loss of primary S-band RF downlink capability on Viking Lander 2; mission forced to rely on backup TWTA No. 2 and UHF relay link, reducing communication redundancy and operational flexibility during extended mission operations on Mars.

evidence.quote: stepping or incremental commands...simply cause the device to move by the commanded increment from its current position. If the current position is incorrect, the next position and all subsequent positions will be incorrect.
explicit commands do not depend on the success of previous commands to be successful, they tend to be 'fault-tolerant,' in the sense that they do not perpetuate errors
When a power problem occurred...the scan platform moved inadvertently...the platform then had to be commanded by trial-and-error until Mars appeared

impact_notes: Mariner '69: Loss of engineering telemetry and near-total loss of science sequence at Mars encounter. Viking Orbiter '75: Loss of multiple image frames due to filter wheel position error requiring ground recovery.

evidence.quote: Design of spacecraft antennas must include careful attention to amplitude and phase characteristics of the combined antenna system pattern, rather than to those of the individual elements alone.
The resultant signal, as measured using a mockup, had combined amplitude, phase and frequency modulation, which had not been considered in the link design.

impact_notes: Communication link degradation affecting uplink/downlink command and telemetry channels; marginal carrier tracking condition created operational risk during critical mission phases.

evidence.quote: Fuses for the Data Storage Subsystem (DSS) tape recorder and several other subsystems were located within the power subsystem...To replace the fuse, the entire unit had to be returned to the vendor for 7-10 days.

Fuses should be located within the device being fused, rather than concentrating them within the power subsystem.

evidence.quote: It was understood a priority that the gyro output would "saturate" during launch, and that this saturation output would be at equal limiting values, thus ensuring a valid logic comparison. Instead, however, the output oscillated significant

evidence.quote: Multiple vehicle interferences can occur when modulation on the carrier of one Viking channel produces sideband energy, which extends into a second channel.
Compatibility testing was accomplished at Compatibility Test Area 21 (CTA 21). This testing identified the most severe (likely) interference condition as false uplink RF acquisition.

evidence.quote: A system design must include requirements for "testability." Test requirements may create "abnormal" operating modes which are easy to overlook during the design of individual elements of a system.

evidence.quote: interface circuit between the Voyager CCS and Power subsystems was not electrically isolated, spacecraft system noise prevented reliable commanding
not discovered until system-level test, and circuit modifications were difficult

evidence.quote: The power switching relays were arranged in a matrix...when two relays are commanded simultaneously, two columns and two rows are enabled at the same time, permitting up to four relays to be actuated.

evidence.quote: Short-term tests of RF circuits may not be sufficient to rule out zenering, and testing may be complicated by 'healing' during the off period.

evidence.quote: During final flight acceptance testing, an RF breakdown in an exciter revealed that its design was subject to RF breakdown at critical pressure.

evidence.quote: Likely causes were corrosion or residue from propellant migration or pyro valve blowby, or particulate contamination.

evidence.quote: Deployment clearance verification testing should be repeated if any related changes are incorporated.

evidence.quote: hermetic connectors purchased were not specified on the LaRC PR to be shipped with the inserts installed

evidence.quote: ion pump was purchased to be the maintaining pump on a chamber before all vapor and leak loads within the chamber were calculated (or measured)

evidence.quote: It is possible to not have a 5:1 ratio of N2 to O2 in a vacuum chamber that has a steady leak, when there is a cryopump running that will preferentially pump the O2.

evidence.quote: The original EMI filters on MIDAS were not matched with the power converter, and were not successful in keeping the EMI below the required level.

evidence.quote: MIDAS EMI testing was only done at the experiment assembly level, and when problems were found it required extensive re-work.

evidence.quote: If a system is baked with the ion pump off, the contaminants driven off of the chamber walls during baking may be driven into the pump, and it will then take quite a while to be able to restart it.

impact_notes: Ion pump failure and extended restart time during baking operations; operational inefficiency and potential mission delays due to improper procedure execution.

evidence.quote: A wire harness was installed on a flight unit for a fit check. Subsequent manufacturing operations were continued through several steps until it was realized that the wire harness was meant to be a temporary installation only.

relying only on procedures to control an undesired event can result in a catastrophic failure. A hard stop (or an interlock) should have limited the cowl's travel to the maximum planned cowl angle.

evidence.quote: Starting voltages in excess of 400 volts during orbital arc welding may propagate at significant levels across interfaces into electronics. Some electronic devices may be sensitive to stray voltage levels as low as a fraction of a volt.

Initiation of the weld arc uses starting voltages as high as 40,000 volts, with resultant stray voltages on the tubing in excess of 400 volts. This could propagate at significant levels across interfaces into electronics.

impact_notes: Risk to sensitive spacecraft electronics (detectors) from electrical overstress; no reported failures where mitigation procedures were applied, but potential for mission-critical hardware damage.

impact_notes: Type C fire incident at Stennis Space Center test facility during construction operations; fire resulted from hot welding slag igniting combustibles due to procedural non-compliance and inadequate supervision.

evidence.quote: The MER Flight Control Team (FCT) was fully formed only one month prior to the June 2003 launch...did not participate in the development of the Mission Operations System (MOS) and the Ground Data System (GDS).
The FCT was not trained and certified in time for launch operations, and it had insufficient time to become familiar with the spacecraft and project procedures.
The FCT was not involved in the decision to change the naming convention...This increases the risk that the FCT may send erroneous commands to the spacecraft.

impact_notes: Late FCT formation and lack of ops involvement in MOS/GDS development created critical mission risk: untrained personnel, inadequate procedures, and command transmission errors that could compromise spacecraft operations.

evidence.quote: The Mission Operations System (MOS) funding in Phase D (integration & test), however, was inadequate to support full implementation of the recommended OSS system.
Late procurement of a server system comprised of low-end, poorly integrated hardware incapable of meeting mission requirements for speed and reliability.
Very limited testing prior to operational use because the original OSS was not installed until late in system integration and test (ATLO).

impact_notes: Mission-critical server failures weeks prior to MER-A launch and EDL; required complete system replacement just before EDL operations; posed direct mission risk to both rover missions.

evidence.quote: testing is interrupted and test time is lost because of unreported changes to the testbed configuration
test operator had been unaware that the previous shift had removed the Rover camera from the testbed
system development continues even while testing is underway in dual work shifts

impact_notes: Two hours of test time lost per incident; increased risk of flight hardware damage; schedule delays on time-constrained projects.

evidence.quote: Each newly assembled flight team's initial inexperience with the flight system. Inadequate review of command products to assure that all checks were performed. An incomplete command uplink process (e.g., missing procedural steps).

The root causes of in-flight commanding errors include the erosion of Flight Team technical expertise upon the transition from completed projects to new missions...and the tendency to short-cut established processes under schedule pressure.

Schedule periodic Flight Team training in command processes, including an overview of the uplink process, discussion of lessons learned...Utilize the full capabilities of the flight system testbed; when in doubt, err on the side of too much

impact_notes: Uplink command errors during critical mission phases could cause loss of instrument or spacecraft. Multiple missions affected (MGS, Stardust, Mars Odyssey, Genesis) with 41 total command errors across 4,028 command files radiated.

evidence.quote: technical content and review standards were diluted as the program accelerated and budgets adjusted
Technical products were compromised by abandoning established processes, tools, and standards of performance

impact_notes: Program implementation impacted by diluted technical standards and abandoned established processes; requirements creep and specification gaps delayed development and increased contractor rework burden.

evidence.quote: Project formulation is often the first thing to be sacrificed as funding is allocated, schedule pressures build, and contracts are initiated.

impact_notes: Final proposal 58% higher than government estimates ($48-52M baseline); cost overrun driven by inadequate formulation and underappreciated technical/programmatic differences from prior X-43A effort.

evidence.quote: Program had not fully achieved one integrated master schedule linking all NGLT projects with standardized schedule formats at the time of Program closing.

impact_notes: Program closure delayed or compromised due to inability to track integrated schedule across merged programs; risk of schedule visibility gaps and milestone tracking failures affecting downstream projects.

evidence.quote: Dynamic inversion controller contributes significantly to cross-coupled response in presence of surface failures (locked surface).

While testing the original dynamic inverse controller with locked stabilator failure simulations, significant lateral acceleration (ny) and angle of sideslip (Beta) excursions as a result of lateral stick inputs were noted.

Because subsequent re-designs of the neural network were not able to modify this behavior and pilot comments from simulation sessions continued to be negative, it was decided that the research controller be modified.

impact_notes: Control law instability under failure conditions (locked control surfaces) required redesign of research controller architecture, delaying Gen 2 flight test implementation and necessitating hybrid controller approach.

evidence.quote: Project staffs do not have cost-estimating experience and NASA independent cost estimates are lacking. Contributing factors to poor cost estimates are immature understanding of complex requirements, transition to a full-cost environment, 'n
Contract proposal estimates came in more than 50% over these estimates. This created significant problems for NASA and the USAF, ultimately contributing to its cancellation.
Major procurements should not be awarded before such a cost assessment is accomplished.

impact_notes: Project cancellation due to cost estimate inaccuracy exceeding 50%; schedule slips and de-scope of content resulted from cost underestimation.

evidence.quote: The X-43C Project budget and milestones were established well ahead of detailed cost and schedule analysis. Per Office of Aerospace Technology (OAT) policy at that time, no budget reserves or schedule margins were allowed.
Inability to adjust the spending profile forced the project to rearrange cost elements to match the profile, adding risk and extending the schedule.
Lack of effective reserves, complicated by OAT 1-year spending metrics, exacerbated the problem.

impact_notes: Schedule slip of first flight; forced rearrangement of cost elements added project risk and extended schedule due to incompatible spending profile and lack of budget reserves.

evidence.quote: Budget errors, omissions, and inconsistencies during formulation, coupled with the need for a focal point to respond to and coordinate with Headquarters Management and Center Chief Financial Office (CFO) personnel.

Programs should establish a central resources integration office and baseline the budget as quickly as possible, using a formal mechanism for tracking and approving changes (e.g., NGLT Program Requirements Control Board (PRCB)).

Having a program integration team as a single focal point for Headquarters and the Centers was critical...allowing the program integration office to field questions and inquiries from Headquarters and Centers.

impact_notes: Budget errors, omissions, and inconsistencies created coordination inefficiencies and resource management challenges across program formulation; impact on schedule and cost control through lack of centralized tracking and change control.

evidence.quote: perform a Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) on GSE test equipment to identify failure modes that could cause test monitoring and control software to initiate an overtest

GSE power meter stopped taking measurements, possibly due to an AC power anomaly. The test monitoring software then interpreted the zero reading...as a loss of power, and responded by increasing the drive level
an attenuator or limiter was inserted to maintain the RF output below a specified level, preventing the TWTA from providing more power than the maximum rating

evidence.quote: GIFTS did not have an instrument co-registration requirement, except that the co-registration be measurable and be determined from the ground data.

evidence.quote: the apparent downlink signal degradation tends to elicit consternation in a critical phase of the mission if these expected effects have not been thoroughly coordinated between the Flight Project and the Deep Space Network

Saturation effects in the station's receiver software algorithms contribute to the indications of signal degradation

impact_notes: No actual telemetry or ranging signal degradation; however, false indications caused consternation during critical mission phases (initial acquisition) for multiple missions (MER-A, Mars Odyssey), risking unnecessary operational responses and mission timeline impacts.

evidence.quote: The hot operating temperature limit imposed by the ERD was found to pertain solely to the extreme low temperature science data collection periods. The ERD did not specify environmental limits for ambient, "non-science" operating conditions

there should be no ambiguity between the various project life-cycle environments defined in the ERD. Do not assume that all life-cycle environments (including supposedly benign environments) are enveloped by the specification

evidence.quote: its antenna was not secured. During the parachute deployment, the forces and accelerations that occurred caused the antenna to break off

evidence.quote: The excessive length of the main chute retention line causes the main chute to be pulled out of the rocket airframe when the drogue chute is deployed at apogee during the first rocket launch.

During the second rocket launch, the main chute retention line was shortened in an attempt to solve this problem. However, it still contained enough slack to cause the main chute bag to exit the rocket airframe.
Excessive slack in the main chute retention line can cause inadvertent main chute deployment or main chute bag entanglement.

impact_notes: Two flight test failures: first rocket descended on main chute from apogee instead of drogue; second rocket descended under drogue only due to main chute entanglement, both preventing nominal recovery sequence and risking loss of flight data and vehicle.

evidence.quote: misreading of part IDs resulted in numerous problem reports, time-consuming paperwork, and lost man-hours to correct the discrepancies. This occurred in 10 to 20 percent of all TCS removals.
entering data by visual observation and handwritten entries produced numerous errors; but by bar-coding parts, a database can be built and used for easy processing of thousands of parts.

time-consuming efforts in inventory control and reporting functions; mundane repetitive accountability procedures; the need for more stringent requirements for accuracy and quality control; and problems with configuration control and docume

evidence.quote: The operating personnel used several different indicators to determine when the rotator was unloaded; however, all of the methods depended on feel or sight. Less experienced operating personnel were not always able to make the right call.

evidence.quote: no Engineering Management processes were exercised to provide control [of LCC objectives]
systems requirements must be clearly defined, flowed down, and controlled during the design development and test evaluation phases

evidence.quote: The requirements did not address unplanned purge outages or times when excursions outside the purge parameters occurred.

evidence.quote: point leak checks of individual threaded, flange, brazed, and welded joints...do not cover all possible sources of propellant leakage. Fatigue or wear during a previous flight or damage during ground processing could result in leakage from

evidence.quote: For many years Ground Support Equipment (GSE) was periodically proof loaded based on NSTS 08171...This caused premature wear of components, decreased safety, and increased time to perform maintenance.

evidence.quote: highly polished sealing surfaces of Dynatube fittings are susceptible to incurring defects...probability of incurring a defect is compounded in designs with a limited envelope for access, or where repetitive disconnections/connections are r

evidence.quote: The established way to remove these damaged materials was by hand with pneumatic tools. This method was dangerous and time-consuming, and it caused significant damage to the underlying reinforcement steel.

evidence.quote: Having the FCP obtain the electrical power it needs to run its pumps from the spacecraft's electrical system creates an inherent risk to fuel cell operation.

an FCP was inadvertently removed from the electrical bus while the FCP was still operating, resulting in a loss of electrical power to the FCP's pumps. With the FCP's pumps no longer operating, the cooling system also went down.
if the temperature of the coolant exceeds approximately 400 °F, it will break down into extremely toxic compounds

having information on system performance on hand can reduce the need for expensive and time-consuming testing or modeling, can reduce the number of assumptions made

evidence.quote: Performing internal leak checks of each component during every orbiter processing flow was not only time-consuming, but used a great deal of test equipment and required technicians to enter the vehicle on multiple occasions, which increased

evidence.quote: Commercial DC/DC converters were tested to the full levels of MIL-PRF-38534 without consideration of the datasheet limitations, thus overstressing the parts.

MIL-PRF-38534 demands a 3000g constant acceleration test...However, the specification sheet indicates a tolerance to 500g constant acceleration...There was no relevance of a 3000g constant acceleration test for the usage of these parts.

Hybrid DC/DC converters have large magnetics connected to an alumina substrate that is subject to wear under excessive mechanical stress, so this is the most likely explanation for premature wear of the parts.

impact_notes: Mission ended approximately 4 years after launch when a critical converter failed; multiple on-orbit failures occurred over 1-4 year duration due to premature part wear from overtesting.

evidence.quote: Spacecraft aerodynamic instability and reaction thruster off-axis skewing greatly reduce contingency or backup options for safe return of crew in the event of a single common flight control computer failure.

If a vehicle is unstable transonically and cannot initiate or continue a safe reentry without active computer control...reentry can be difficult, if not impossible, to recover from common mode software or hardware flight computer failures.

Inherently unstable spacecraft configurations, skewed thruster configurations, and lack of manual controls and displays require reliance on-board flight control computers which bring the inherent risk of common software failure.

impact_notes: Loss of crew during reentry if common-mode software/computer failure occurs in aerodynamically unstable spacecraft with no manual control backup or non-computer contingency options.
